title: coleus grandidentatus
intro: Coleus grandidentatus, also known as Coleus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its active compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Coleus grandidentatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant protection, making it a valuable herb in various herbal preparations. Coleus grandidentatus is often used in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to skin conditions such as wounds and eczema.
